user_query:
I have a small set of 3D orientations encoded in mixed representations. Please do the following:

1) Read input/rotations.json. Interpret quaternions using the Hamilton convention [w, x, y, z]. Interpret Euler angles using the intrinsic 'zyx' sequence (yaw, pitch, roll in radians). For axis-angle, treat the axis as a unit vector.

2) Convert every entry to a 3x3 rotation matrix and validate it as a proper SO(3) rotation (orthogonality and determinant≈1 within a tolerance of 1e-6). Record which indices are valid and which are invalid.

3) Using only the valid rotations, compute an average rotation using a proper SO(3) averaging method (do not average matrix elements directly). Report this average in three forms: 3x3 matrix, quaternion [w,x,y,z], and intrinsic 'zyx' Euler angles (radians). Also report the mean rotation’s rotation vector (Log map).

4) Compute the relative rotation that maps the first valid rotation to the last valid rotation. Report this relative rotation as: 3x3 matrix, quaternion [w,x,y,z], axis-angle (axis and angle), rotation vector (Log map), and intrinsic 'zyx' Euler angles (radians).

5) For each valid rotation, compute its rotation vector (Log map) and write them to output/rotvecs.csv with header: index,rx,ry,rz. Please format each numeric value in this CSV with at least 6 decimal places.

6) Write a machine-readable summary to output/report.json with this structure:
{
  "conventions": {"quaternion": "wxyz", "euler_axes": "zyx", "euler_intrinsic": true},
  "valid_indices": [...],
  "invalid_indices": [...],
  "valid_count": <int>,
  "invalid_count": <int>,
  "mean": {
    "matrix": [[...],[...],[...]],
    "quaternion_wxyz": [w,x,y,z],
    "euler_zyx": [z,y,x],
    "rotvec": [rx,ry,rz]
  },
  "relative_first_to_last_valid": {
    "matrix": [[...],[...],[...]],
    "quaternion_wxyz": [w,x,y,z],
    "axis": [ax,ay,az],
    "angle": <float>,
    "rotvec": [rx,ry,rz],
    "euler_zyx": [z,y,x]
  }
}

7) Add a short human-readable audit summary to output/diagnostics.txt explaining: the validation checks (orthogonality & determinant) and tolerances used, the averaging approach (e.g., SO(3) mean using Log/Exp or quaternion-based), and a brief note on any invalid entries.

Keep numeric computations stable and consistent with the stated conventions. Save all outputs to the output/ directory exactly as specified.
